title: Error: unable to find connection when removing a machine that was init with --ignition-path
body:
### Issue Description

If a Podman machine was created using a custom ignition file `podman machine init --igntion-path <path>`, then this error will appear when trying to remove the machine:

```
podman machine rm with-ign
The following files will be deleted:


/Users/gaufde/.config/containers/podman/machine/applehv/with-ign.json
/var/folders/7m/z_f3vkmd6t30fhydp4z513gw0000gn/T/podman/with-ign.sock
/var/folders/7m/z_f3vkmd6t30fhydp4z513gw0000gn/T/podman/with-ign-gvproxy.sock
/var/folders/7m/z_f3vkmd6t30fhydp4z513gw0000gn/T/podman/with-ign-api.sock
/var/folders/7m/z_f3vkmd6t30fhydp4z513gw0000gn/T/podman/with-ign.log
Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
Error: failed to remove machines files: unable to find connection named "with-ign"
```



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine init --igntion-path <path> <name>` (FYI this won't work in podman 5.2.4, see https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23544).
2. `podman machine rm <name>`


### Describe the results you received

The error can present itself in both of these ways:
```
podman machine rm <name>
Error: failed to remove machines files: unable to find connection named "<name>"
```

or 

```
podman machine reset

Are you sure you want to continue? [y/N] y
Error: 1 error occurred:
	* unable to find connection named "<name>"
```


### Describe the results you expected

No error

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
